Hub Home > legal-and-compliance > Terms of Service Generator

Terms of Service Generator

Generate comprehensive Terms of Service for a website, SaaS product, or e-commerce store — covering acceptable use, payment terms, refund policy, intellectual property, liability limitations, and dispute resolution.

Intermediate PASTE & GO Risk-reduction
Pro tip

Your Terms of Service should be specific to how YOUR product works, not a generic template. If you offer subscriptions, address auto-renewal. If you have user-generated content, address content ownership. Generic ToS leave gaps that expose you to risk.

terms of service tos legal compliance user agreement website legal

Prompt Variants by Model

Claude Claude 4.x
FRESH APR 2026 
You are a business attorney drafting Terms of Service. Write for the specific business model described, not a generic template.

<business_model>
Business: [NAME] — [WHAT YOU DO]
Website/app:...
You are a business attorney drafting Terms of Service. Write for the specific business model described, not a generic template.

<business_model>
Business: [NAME] — [WHAT YOU DO]
Website/app:...

Unlock this prompt + all 74 in the library — free, updated monthly, zero spam.

Your email unlocks all 74 prompts instantly. No password, no signup — just your email. 

You are a business attorney drafting Terms of Service. Write for the specific business model described, not a generic template.

<business_model>
Business: [NAME] — [WHAT YOU DO]
Website/app: [URL]
Business type: [SAAS / E-COMMERCE / SERVICE / MARKETPLACE / CONTENT/MEDIA]
Location: [COUNTRY/STATE — determines governing law]
Revenue model: [SUBSCRIPTION / ONE-TIME / FREEMIUM / FREE — describe specifics]
User accounts: [YES/NO — what can users do when logged in]
User-generated content: [YES/NO — can users post, upload, or submit content]
Payment processor: [STRIPE / PAYPAL / etc.]
Refund policy: [DESCRIBE YOUR POLICY — or "help me create one"]
</business_model>

Draft Terms of Service covering:

1. **Acceptance of terms** — how agreeing works (click-wrap, browse-wrap)
2. **Eligibility** — age requirements, geographic restrictions
3. **Account responsibilities** — registration, security, termination
4. **Acceptable use** — what users can and cannot do (be specific to your platform)
5. **Payment and billing** — pricing, billing cycle, auto-renewal disclosure, taxes
6. **Refunds and cancellation** — specific policy with timeframes and process
7. **Intellectual property** — your content ownership, user content licensing, DMCA process if applicable
8. **User-generated content** (if applicable) — content standards, your right to moderate/remove, user grants
9. **Third-party links and services** — disclaimer of responsibility
10. **Disclaimer of warranties** — "as is" provision
11. **Limitation of liability** — cap on damages
12. **Indemnification** — user holds you harmless
13. **Dispute resolution** — governing law, mandatory arbitration or litigation, class action waiver
14. **Modifications** — how you will notify users of changes
15. **Contact information**

Write in plain English. Bold key obligations and restrictions so users can scan. End with AI disclaimer.
Notes: Claude tailors each section to the specific business model. The business type selection drives which sections get expanded or simplified.
ChatGPT GPT-5
FRESH APR 2026 
Act as a business attorney. Draft Terms of Service specific to my business model.

**My Business:**
- Name: [NAME] — [WHAT WE DO]
- Website:...

**My Business:**
- Name: [NAME] — [WHAT WE DO]
- Website:...

Your email unlocks all 74 prompts instantly. No password, no signup — just your email. 

Act as a business attorney. Draft Terms of Service specific to my business model.

**My Business:**
- Name: [NAME] — [WHAT WE DO]
- Website: [URL]
- Type: [SAAS/E-COMMERCE/SERVICE/MARKETPLACE/CONTENT]
- Location: [COUNTRY/STATE]
- Revenue: [SUBSCRIPTION/ONE-TIME/FREEMIUM/FREE]
- User accounts: [YES/NO, what they do]
- User-generated content: [YES/NO]
- Payments via: [PROCESSOR]
- Refund policy: [DESCRIBE OR "help me create one"]

**Cover:** Acceptance, eligibility, accounts, acceptable use, payments, refunds, IP, user content (if applicable), third-party links, warranty disclaimer, liability cap, indemnification, dispute resolution, modifications, contact.

Plain English. Bold key obligations. End with AI disclaimer.
Notes: Custom GPT with your business model pre-loaded for consistent ToS updates as your product evolves.

I need Terms of Service for my website/product.

**Business:**
- Name: [NAME] — [WHAT WE DO]
- Website: [URL]
- Type: [SAAS/E-COMMERCE/SERVICE/MARKETPLACE]
- Location: [COUNTRY/STATE]
- Revenue model: [DESCRIBE]
- User accounts: [YES/NO]
- User content: [YES/NO]
- Payments: [PROCESSOR]
- Refund policy: [DESCRIBE OR "help me create one"]

**Cover:** Acceptance, eligibility, accounts, acceptable use, payments, refunds, IP, user content, third parties, warranty disclaimer, liability, indemnification, disputes, modifications, contact.

Plain English with bolded key terms. End with AI disclaimer.
Notes: Gemini can research competitor ToS for comparison and industry-standard terms.

- I am based in:...
Help me write Terms of Service for my website.

**My business:**
- Name: [NAME] — [WHAT WE DO]
- Website: [URL]
- Type: [ONLINE STORE / SOFTWARE / SERVICE / MARKETPLACE]
- I am based in:...

Unlock this prompt + all 74 in the library — free, updated monthly, zero spam.

Your email unlocks all 74 prompts instantly. No password, no signup — just your email. 

Help me write Terms of Service for my website.

**My business:**
- Name: [NAME] — [WHAT WE DO]
- Website: [URL]
- Type: [ONLINE STORE / SOFTWARE / SERVICE / MARKETPLACE]
- I am based in: [COUNTRY/STATE]
- How I charge: [SUBSCRIPTION/ONE-TIME/FREE/FREEMIUM]
- Do people create accounts: [YES/NO — what can they do]
- Can users post or upload content: [YES/NO]
- Payment processor: [STRIPE/PAYPAL/etc.]
- My refund policy: [DESCRIBE — or "help me figure this out"]

**Include sections about:**
How people agree to the terms, who can use the site, account rules, what users can and cannot do, payment and billing, refunds and cancellations, who owns the content, user-posted content rules (if applicable), links to other sites, no guarantees, limits on what I owe if something goes wrong, how to handle disputes, how I will tell users about changes, and how to contact me.

Plain language. Bold the most important parts. End with a note that this is AI-generated and needs lawyer review.
Notes: Copilot in Word formats the ToS as a professional legal document.
